scope.summary: Swift structured concurrency runtime API attribution now reports Task.sleep, Task.yield, and task-group calls through the shared scheduling, timer, aggregate, cancellation/liveness, and exception-channel obligations.

capability_alignment.capability: map Swift stdlib concurrency evidence onto existing cross-language async runtime obligations
not_a_feature_list: No new SourceProtocolKind, SourceCallKind, semantic-pack row, or public kernel API was added.
No exact admission path was opened for Swift async runtime APIs.
Task.sleep uses timer scheduling plus task cancellation/liveness obligations instead of sync value equivalence.
Task.yield uses a Swift-neutral task-yield scheduling obligation instead of reusing the JS scheduler.yield microtask label.
Task-group calls use aggregate completion, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, and throwing exception-channel obligations while exact recovery remains closed.
reused_capabilities: Swift source-backed call surfaces lowered as ordinary call nodes
shared timer, task, async aggregate, cancellation/liveness, and exception-channel missing-evidence labels

new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The callee root must be an unshadowed Task name, with no same-file or corpus-visible Swift Task definition.
The callee root must be an unshadowed Task name, with no same-file or corpus-visible Swift Task definition.
The callee must be an unshadowed Swift free function name with no same-file or corpus-visible definition for that task-group symbol.

assertions: Task.sleep reports timer scheduling and task cancellation/liveness obligations
withTaskGroup reports all-completion, cancellation/liveness, and result-channel obligations
withThrowingTaskGroup also reports the exception-channel obligation
withDiscardingTaskGroup reports completion and cancellation/liveness without a result-channel obligation
withThrowingDiscardingTaskGroup reports the exception-channel obligation without a result-channel obligation
A local Task binding blocks Task.sleep obligation attribution
A Task extension member blocks Task.sleep obligation attribution
A same-file withTaskGroup function blocks task-group aggregate attribution
A project-visible Task extension member blocks Task.sleep obligation attribution
A project-visible Swift withTaskGroup function blocks task-group aggregate attribution
Task.sleep and Task.yield appear as interpretable admission rejections with specific obligation subreasons
Task-group calls appear in oracle-excluded runtime units with aggregate and exception-channel attribution

corpus_pricing_delta.reason: The pricing script now counts Swift Task.sleep, Task.yield, and task-group surfaces as reporting-supported closed boundaries, and corrects Task.detached(...) pricing that was already supported by the runtime reporter.

release_verify_timing.assessment: No large degradation observed; warm repeats are within normal run-to-run noise of the prior 0.96s reference.

next_exact_work_policy.summary: Swift structured concurrency is now attributed precisely enough for recall-loss reporting, but exact recovery still requires dependency-closed scheduling, cancellation, task lifecycle, aggregate result-channel, actor/executor, and callback/effect contracts.
